---
title: "X/Twitter"
platform_slug: "x"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/regional/BR/x/ugc.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/x/ugc.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/x/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/x/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/x/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/x/ads.yml
---

X's UGC transparency is assessed through the [X API](https://docs.x.com/x-api/getting-started/about-x-api) across Brazil, the EU, and the UK, while advertising transparency relies on the [X Ads Repository](https://ads.twitter.com/ads-repository), available only in the EU under DSA obligations. On the UGC dimension, the platform scores in the Restricted band across all regions: the API exposes public post data and performs well on secondary criteria covering data format compliance, endpoint coverage, consistent data returns, and near-real-time availability. However, API access is not free of charge and no graphical interface for UGC data extraction exists. On advertising, X scores at or near the Irrelevant band in Brazil and the UK, where no ad repository is provided; in the EU, despite the X Ads Repository launched under DSA obligations, API access was not granted during the evaluation period and the GUI consistently returned empty CSV files, rendering the repository non-functional in practice. The resulting profile places X as a platform with moderate UGC transparency constrained by cost barriers and with advertising transparency that is effectively unavailable outside the EU and non-functional within it.

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("x")
```




## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="x",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="x",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```
